content.verdict: The Dunmore is the most polished of Harbour Island's bluff-top hotels — a 14-acre estate of pastel-blue and white cottages designed by interior icon Amanda Lindroth, with a private path down to Pink Sands Beach. The hotel has the preppy, old-Florida-meets-Bahamas character that has made Harbour Island a longtime favorite of New York and Palm Beach society. The Dunmore is intimate, polished, and perfectly located.
content.best_room: The Dunmore's accommodation is split between Cottages (set in the gardens) and Suites (in the main building). The Beachfront Cottages with private gardens are the best for honeymooners — true standalone privacy with quick access to Pink Sands Beach. The new Lindroth-designed Garden Suites offer the freshest interiors. Cottage 12 is consistently the most requested for its position closest to the beach path.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival on Harbour Island
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly to North Eleuthera, taxi to the Three Island Dock, and water-taxi 10 minutes across to Harbour Island. The Dunmore's golf cart meets the dock. Check in at the iconic blue and white main building, settle into your cottage, walk the bluff-top path down to Pink Sands Beach for first sunset. Dinner at the Dunmore's beachfront restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Pink Sands Beach Day
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Dunmore has a private path down to Pink Sands Beach with chairs, umbrellas, and lunch service set up daily on the dune line. Spend the full day on the iconic pink sand. Lunch at the beach pavilion (the Dunmore's Bahama Mama and grilled grouper sandwich are local legends). Sunset cocktails at the bluff-top pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Dunmore Town Walking Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Dunmore Town is one of the oldest settlements in the Bahamas and the only town on Harbour Island. A morning walk through the pastel Loyalist cottage streets — Bay Street, Pitt Street, the Royal Cemetery overlooking the harbor. Lunch at Sip Sip on the bluff for the famous conch chowder. Afternoon ice cream at Arthur's Bakery, sunset cocktails at the harbor.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa & Boutique Shopping
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at the Dunmore's spa for a couples treatment, then a mid-day exploring the small but excellent boutiques along Bay Street — Lulu Lemon, Sugar Mill (the Lindroth-designed shop), and Beach House for resort wear. Late afternoon back at the pool. Dinner at the Landing on the harbor for elegant Bahamian-Mediterranean cuisine and sunset over the marina.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snorkeling Devil's Backbone
itinerary_7_nights.description: Half-day snorkeling charter to Devil's Backbone reef and the surrounding cays — multiple shipwrecks lie on this notoriously dangerous reef, now beautiful under the water. The Cienfuegos wreck is the highlight. Lunch on the boat or at a sandbar, afternoon back at the Dunmore for pool time and Pink Sands sunset.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Eleuthera Day Trip
itinerary_7_nights.description: Cross to Eleuthera for the day. Rent a car and drive to the Glass Window Bridge — the dramatic spot where the deep blue Atlantic meets the calm turquoise Caribbean separated by a thin strip of road. Continue to Preacher's Cave (where Eleuthera was first colonized) and Queen's Bath natural pools. Return to Harbour Island by late afternoon.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Pink Sand Morning & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: A final morning walk on Pink Sands Beach — the soft pink color is most striking at sunrise. Breakfast at the Dunmore's terrace, golf cart back to the dock, water taxi to North Eleuthera for the connection home. The Dunmore's combination of polish and small scale leaves a strong impression.

caveats: The Dunmore is on the bluff above Pink Sands Beach — direct beach access requires a short walk down a private path; couples wanting room-to-sand access in seconds should look at properties directly on the dune line.
Harbour Island's small scale means restaurant tables and high-demand boutiques fill quickly in peak season; reservations are essential.
Travel logistics involve a connection through Nassau or seasonal Florida flights to North Eleuthera, then a water taxi — total journey 6 to 8 hours from US East Coast cities.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: The Dunmore is exceptional for honeymooners who want a polished, intimate hotel on Harbour Island — the Lindroth-designed interiors, the bluff-top setting, the private path to Pink Sands Beach, and the easy walk to Dunmore Town's restaurants make it the best base on the island for couples who want sophistication with charm.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: November through May is the dry season. December through April is peak. The hotel closes briefly in late summer/early fall for the deepest hurricane months. February through April is the most reliably beautiful weather.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: The Dunmore is small — fewer than 30 rooms — and books out 4 to 6 months ahead for peak season. Beachfront Cottages and the renovated Garden Suites are the first to fill. Holiday weeks require near-year-ahead booking.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: The Dunmore is not adults-only but skews heavily adult and couple. The 14-acre estate, the polished aesthetic, and the size mean families with young children are uncommon. The atmosphere is consistently grown-up.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Beachfront Cottages with private gardens — particularly Cottage 12 — are the most requested for honeymooners. The new Lindroth-designed Garden Suites offer the freshest interiors. Both deliver the property's signature character.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: North Eleuthera airport, a 5-minute taxi to Three Island Dock, then 10-minute water taxi to Harbour Island. The Dunmore arranges golf cart transfers from the dock. Direct seasonal flights from Florida to North Eleuthera reduce total transit time.
